title: Checkout silently does nothing when the API response lacks a checkout URL
reasoning: The handler only redirects when checkout_url is truthy. If the endpoint returns a successful response with a missing, null, or malformed checkout_url, no error is shown; the finally block clears loading and the user is left on the page with no explanation. The typed generic does not validate the runtime response shape.
reproduction: Mock apiRequest to resolve to {} or { checkout_url: '' } and click the checkout button after accepting terms; loading stops and no error is displayed.
recommendation: Validate that checkout_url is a non-empty string before redirecting and throw or set a user-facing error when it is absent.
whyTestsDoNotAlreadyCoverThis: No linked tests were provided for malformed checkout responses or checkout error states.
suggestedRegressionTest: Add a component/unit test that mocks apiRequest returning an object without checkout_url and asserts that an error message is rendered.
minimumFixScope: handleGeoCheckout response validation and error message handling in frontend/app/geo/page.tsx.
